stemHtml: An aircraft making an approach at night has been flying over terrain and turns toward the aerodrome, where the runway faces the sea. The aircraft is flying below the glideslope, and the Pilot Monitoring calls for a go-around. If, during the go-around, Pilot Flying looks outside the aircraft rather than relying on instruments, which of the following illusions may occur?

options.a: Somatogravic illusion causing the pilot to pitch up.
options.b: Inversion illusion causing the pilot to pitch up.
options.c: Inversion illusion causing the pilot to pitch down.
options.d: Somatogravic illusion causing the pilot to pitch down.
